text.title: Cannibalization Detection SOP Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Cannibalization Detection SOP` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: The report MUST contain a Search-Console-sourced query → ranking-page mapping for the top 200 queries by clicks. Hand-collected mappings forbidden.
rule.rationale: Hand-collected mappings miss the long-tail; SC-sourced data is the only complete picture of which page ranks for what.

rule.statement: Each cannibalization candidate MUST carry an intent-overlap score (0-3: distinct / partial / heavy / identical) with rationale. Distinct (0) candidates are dropped from the report.
rule.rationale: Bare URL-overlap surfaces false positives; intent scoring filters out the legitimate dual-coverage cases.

rule.statement: Each surviving candidate MUST carry a recommendation: consolidate (one canonical, 301 the other) OR de-optimize (narrow the loser's intent). Hand-wave 'optimize' recommendations are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Vague 'optimize' recommendations stall execution; the binary consolidate-or-deopt forces a decision.

rule.statement: Each consolidation recommendation MUST name the engineer who will deploy the 301 redirect and the date. Group handles forbidden.
rule.rationale: Group-owned redirects rot at 80% within 60 days; named owner + date converts to ~70% execution rate.

rule.statement: The report MUST snapshot baseline organic traffic (clicks, impressions, position) per affected URL before consolidation, with a 30-day post-consolidation check scheduled at report-sign-time.
rule.rationale: Without baseline snapshots, post-merge regression goes unmeasured; snapshot + 30-day check makes the win or loss visible.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
